transcript: I want to begin by speaking about the internet's original sin. Um, the internet failed in one particular way, and it's really affected a lot of things that have happened since. The internet could move internet... could could move information but could not move money. It couldn't really move money. Not the way that you could move a message or information. Instant, globally, and without permission. That single omission has really affected how things have been built over the last 30 years, and this industry specifically has focused the last 15 years to correcting that mistake. So, I feel like it's an important point for everything that we work on here to kind of understand that there is a mission behind the work that we do. And kind of fixing that missing piece. So, when we talk about the things that are specific to Solana, and why we are starting to see some change over this work that's been conducted over the last 15 years, uh, we are seeing some of that change on Solana, right? We're seeing the impact of 15 years of decentralized work that can actually be competitive with their centralized counterparties or their centralized competitors. Um, I speak to a lot of blockchain companies, a lot of teams that are building blockchains, a lot of teams that are building products. They are always asking me like what makes Solana special? Why does Solana command the user base that it does? Why is it so popular? You know, why does it do so well on metrics that are important to blockchains, right? Whether it's bridge TVL or DEX volumes, um, or unique wallets or any of those metrics. And it's really hard for me to answer that question because I do look at each blockchain as an economy, and it's very hard to just take one or two aspects of an economy and say that's the reason for their success. Um, so I usually kind of answer it with it really comes down to the people, it comes down to the culture. If you can foster that kind of culture, you will have a lot of success. The way I describe Solana's culture is you've got these rebellious engineers who are mission-driven. They they they believe in a free financial system. They think that that's something worth pursuing. You've got unorthodox quants that are leaving a very lucrative or predictable path in HFT because they believe they can build better markets on-chain. Uh, you've got unapologetic founders that are mission-driven and believe the world will be better with a free and transparent financial system. And you've got unfortunately, yes, degens as well in there. All of those colliding together in the same ecosystem kind of give you, um, I think give you the best shot at creating an economy that's as vibrant as the one on Solana. There are like three things that I think are not the only things, but there are important things that allow for these economies to work. One is how assets come into the space. Two is how users are onboarded. And then three is how capital markets form. I think those three things within an economy, um, are jet fuel for growth. So, if we just look at Wormhole's data, we can see that 30% of transfers end up going to Solana. Solana is the destination chain for those transfers. That's almost $12 billion worth of value that's come into the Solana ecosystem. And behind that value are users, real companies and real people that are looking to, uh, to use the technology and the services and the different products that are being built within that ecosystem. So, you know, by our estimation, there's about 300,000 new users this year that have entered the Solana ecosystem just to use its products, transact on its markets, and and hopefully get some value. Um, the second thing is how users onboard, right? Um, a way a user can come into the Solana ecosystem is by swapping its non-native assets into native assets. Mayan is a protocol that allows for this to happen. And if you've ever used any of the major wallets such as Phantom, Solflare, or Backpack, or you've used a DEX like Raydium or an aggregator like Jupiter, likely if you've swapped from a non-native asset to a native asset, you've probably used Mayan. They've seen over $5 billion worth of value come into the Solana ecosystem just through their product. I think those two things are pretty table stakes at this point. You've got to be able to bring assets in and you've got to be able to onboard users to those applications so that they actually have the right assets to be able to use the products. The last thing I'll talk about is Sunrise. Um, Sunrise is a first of its kind. It was able to demonstrate the formation of capital markets competitively. And the operative word there is competitively. Um, the first asset listing that Sunrise conducted on Solana was the MONAD token. Uh, it took a non-native token that wasn't issued on Solana, brought it to Solana on day one, minute one. Competing not only with all of the other, uh, venues on-chain, but also competing with their centralized competitors, all the centralized exchanges out there. And within the first 24 hours, we saw MONAD spot volumes surpass Hyperliquid's. We saw MONAD's, uh, spot volumes in the first 24 hours surpass even on MONAD itself. Now, that's not a knock on MONAD. MONAD was one day old. But it definitely shows us the power of the user base that's on Solana today. It shows us that tapping into that economy or that market can really make a material difference if you're an asset issuer. Now, for the the statistic that I'm most interested in or I'm most excited about. If Solana were a centralized exchange, it would have been a top five day one centralized exchange for the MONAD listing. It's very hard to do. Anyone who's built decentralized technologies always knows that the centralized counterpart who has full control of the stack, that's living in a data center, um, ha is will outcompete you with speed, in if it's a market-based platform, obviously spreads and liquidity and all these other things. So, for that to happen on-chain, day one, minute one, was not an easy thing to do, but Sunrise was able to do that and really demonstrate to the world that you can have, uh, on-chain capital markets form on the internet and be just as competitive as centralized competitors. And so, when I say that top five, it was a top five centralized exchange listing on-chain, but it beat out Kraken, it beat out KuCoin, it beat out Bitget, it beat out Gate. Like, these are very well-known exchanges that most of us know. Some of these exchanges have million plus user bases. And it was able to be, quote, tighter spreads, it was able to do more volume than those exchanges, all with its native Solana ecosystem user base. So, for me, I get really excited about all of all of the stuff that Sunrise is doing and these listings being possible today on Solana because not not because we're only competing with centralized exchanges, but we're also competing with everything else that's centralized. So if I can demonstrate that a crypto asset can issue on-chain in a decentralized way and it can be just as competitive with its centralized counterparties, it's not just about the crypto token. It can be an equity, it can be a commodity, it can be any financial product. So, that that's really what I think the the big, uh, first use case that we saw with Sunrise and with this listing on Solana. Um, and lastly, I want to just say like the the mission-driven approach for this, right? If if you don't have capital formation happening on-chain, if you don't have price discovery happening on-chain, uh, you're never really going to create that free, transparent, permissionless financial future. You have to have all of these components, uh, uh, in in a competitive way on-chain. Uh, that hasn't been the case traditionally. This was the first time that I saw an on-chain spot listing surpass centralized exchanges. This was the first time I saw an on-chain spot listing that wasn't on its native blockchain list on, uh, on-chain, um, in a in a in a minute one, day one scenario. So, that being said, I'm very excited about what the Solana ecosystem is going to bring. I'm very excited about, uh, what Sunrise, the next listings that Sunrise is going to do. And I'm very excited about capital markets forming on the internet, largely based on Solana. So, thank you so much.

# Wormhole Labs unveils 'Sunrise' gateway to bring MON and other assets to Solana

By [Zack Abrams](https://www.theblock.co/author/zack-abrams)

[Crypto Ecosystems](https://www.theblock.co/category/crypto-ecosystems)
• November 23, 2025, 10:01AM EST

UPDATED: November 23, 2025, 10:09AM EST

Share

Partner offers

The Block may may earn a commission if you use our partner offers, at no extra cost to you.

Start trading cryptoGet a crypto rewards cardGet a crypto loan

#### Quick Take

- Wormhole Labs has launched Sunrise, a liquidity gateway designed to be the “canonical route” for bringing external assets to Solana.
- The platform is launching with immediate support for MON, the native token of the anticipated Monad blockchain, which will go live tomorrow.
- The initiative relies on Wormhole’s Native Token Transfers (NTT) framework to unify liquidity across Solana DEXs like Jupiter and block explorer Orb.

Wormhole Labs, the firm behind the Wormhole cross-chain protocol, today announced the public launch of Sunrise, a new liquidity gateway focused exclusively on the Solana ecosystem.

The product is pitched as a "canonical route" for external assets to enter Solana with day-one liquidity, giving users a single interface to move tokens from their origin chain onto Solana and immediately tap the network’s DeFi venues, according to materials shared with The Block.

According to the announcement, [Sunrise](http://sunrisebridge.xyz) aims to solve the "fragmented" liquidity issues often associated with bridging new tokens, ensuring that new onchain assets are tradable across Solana venues including decentralized exchange Jupiter and supported on Helius-incubated block explorer Orb from day one. Sunrise will launch with day-one support for Monad's [high-profile MON token](https://www.theblock.co/post/380040/monad-token-sale-defies-fizzle-fears-will-end-oversubscribed-on-coinbase), scheduled to begin trading tomorrow, as its first major test case.

"Solana’s vision for internet capital markets means being the platform on which users can engage with any asset, including crypto assets that aren’t originated on Solana," said Kuleen Nimkar, growth lead at the Solana Foundation. "Products like Sunrise are a critical part of enabling this future by giving non-native new assets a seamless, high-liquidity path into the network from day one."

The move comes as interoperability protocols race to capture flows between high-throughput chains.

sources.title: Catalyst: Expansion Notes
sources.snippet:
Catalyst: Expansion Notes | EVE Online
# Catalyst: Expansion Notes
2025-11-14-ByEVE Online Team
[](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https://www.eveonline.com/news/view/catalyst-expansion-notes)[](https://twitter.com/share?url=https://www.eveonline.com/news/view/catalyst-expansion-notes)[](https://vk.com/share.php?url=https://www.eveonline.com/news/view/catalyst-expansion-notes)[](https://www.reddit.com/submit?url=https://www.eveonline.com/news/view/catalyst-expansion-notes)
Link copied
Greetings expansion enthusiasts!
The Catalyst expansion is arriving next week at**11:00 UTC 18 November!**We are delivering the patch notes early so you can absorb everything that's coming next Tuesday. We invite you to join the player discussion about the content of this release on the[EVE Online Discord](https://www.eveonline.com/discord)and the[EVE Online Forums](https://forums.eveonline.com/t/eve-online-catalyst-expansion-notes/500795).
### Expansion Notes 23.02 - EVE Online: Catalyst
[**Map Improvements**](#h2-1)[**Mining UI/UX Improvements**](#h2-2)[**Phased Asteroid Field**](#h2-3)[**Mining Content**](#h2-4)[**New Ships**](#h2-5)[**Modules**](#h2-6)[**Carriers**](#h2-7)[**Balancing**](#h2-8)[**PLEX Transaction Logs**](#h2-9)[**Epic Arc**](#h2-10)## **Map Improvements**
### **New 2D Map**
A brand-new 2D map is now available! You can access it through the 2D toggle button in the bottom-right corner of the map window.
The 2D map comes with two distinct visual modes depending on your zoom level:
* **Heatmap view**when zoomed out, for an overview of the universe data.
* **Tube/Subway-style map**when zoomed in, for a clearer look at systems and connections.
You can also decide how much data to display: use the**eye-shaped toggle**above the 2D button to switch between always-visible data or focusing only around your cursor area.
![](https://images.ctfassets.net/7lhcm73ukv5p/31aqveDTm75FNrEGguL8SZ/b112b6b2e06e7b45b564e008a957e9b8/2dmap2.png?w=850&fm=jpg&fl=progressive&q=75)### **Improved Data Visualisation**
We’ve improved how data is displayed on both the 2D and 3D maps.
The new visualisation now aligns more closely with**player expectations**, drawing inspiration from the classic map’s behaviour. Filters now use**logarithmic scaling,**which makes it easier to distinguish increments in lower-end values, even when big outliers appear on the map.
A**legend**has been added to help interpret the data shown on the map.
By interacting with the**tuning icon**on the legend, you can open the new**filter editing panel**, where you can:
* Adjust the**data range**displayed.
* Switch between**logarithmic**and**linear**scaling.
* Customise the**colours**used for visualisation.
Your changes will be saved per filter, allowing you to tailor how data appears and focus on what’s most relevant to your needs.
![](https://images.ctfassets.net/7lhcm73ukv5p/5PaJuoVEpSbWlLxcvmEBCH/84ebc6a8c7001f4d421cc53939de406b/2dmap3.png?w=850&fm=jpg&fl=progressive&q=75)### **Jump Range Visualisation**
Pilots flying ships equipped with jump drives can now toggle the**jump range**display on or off on both the 3D and 2D map via the**jump icon**in the bottom-right corner.
In the 2D view, reachable systems are highlighted, and in the tube/subway map mode, reachable nodes change their shape from circles to diamonds.
Hovering over a system provides information about**jump range**,**fuel consumption**and**fatigue**incurred after the jump.
### **New Filters**
We’ve expanded the range of filters available on both 2D and 3D maps!
Under the new**Mining**category, you can now choose from:
* **Ore Anomalies**- displayed as pins, showing nearby anomalies within a 5-jump range.
* **Ore Distribution**- showing where ore belts are located across New Eden.
* **Specific Ores**- highlighting systems where particular ores can be found.
This is also where we’re introducing a new**information sidebar**that appears alongside the map. It provides extra context about t

I included the diegetic holographic text '> CEX' to succinctly narrate the core story: on-chain volume surpassing that of Centralized Exchanges, which is the key takeaway from the article.

To the side of the portal, a minimalist, diegetic holographic interface projects the glowing text '> CEX' in a crisp, sans-serif font, as if it were a live status update. In the deep background, monolithic server racks representing old infrastructure are cast in shadow, being eclipsed by the portal's light.

To the side of the awe-inspiring crystalline structure, a minimalist, diegetic holographic interface, sharp and precisely rendered with soft neon mixed lighting, projects the glowing text '> CEX' in a crisp, sans-serif font, as if it were a live status update floating luminously in the digital air. In the deep background, monolithic server racks representing old infrastructure are cast in deep, volumetric shadows, being dramatically eclipsed by the portal's radiant light, creating a high-contrast chiaroscuro effect. The entire scene is ultra-photorealistic, with exceptional detail and intricate textures, composed for a vertical 3:4 aspect ratio, with ample negative space at the top to enhance its towering presence and monumental feel. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, high dynamic range, cinematic.

Emphasize symmetry and central focus with tight macro framing. 8k resolution, octane render, raytracing, physically based rendering (PBR), hyper-detailed, global illumination, cinematic golden-hour lighting, volumetric lighting, ambient occlusion, sharp focus, intricate details.

editorial.headline: Wormhole’s Sunrise Gateway Flips Major CEXs on Monad Volume
editorial.standfirst: The debut of the Sunrise liquidity gateway positioned Solana as a top-five global trading venue for the Monad token launch, surpassing volumes on Kraken and KuCoin. This marks a structural shift where on-chain capital formation now offers deeper liquidity and tighter spreads than centralized incumbents immediately upon issuance.
key_takeaways: Solana captured $12 billion in inbound value via Wormhole, representing 30% of all protocol transfers.
The Monad (MON) listing on Solana generated higher spot volume than Hyperliquid and the Monad native chain in the first 24 hours.
Sunrise enabled Solana to outperform Kraken, KuCoin, and Bitget on spreads and depth for the MON asset launch.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
The narrative regarding blockchain interoperability has shifted from simple asset bridging to competitive capital formation. **Wormhole** has provided definitive data confirming that **Solana** is no longer just a destination for bridged assets but has become a primary liquidity venue capable of outperforming centralized infrastructure. With **Solana** capturing 30% of all Wormhole transfers—representing $12 billion in value—the network has reached a critical mass where it can now dictate price discovery for external assets better than their native environments.

This thesis was validated during the recent **Monad** (MON) token generation event. Utilizing the newly launched **Sunrise** gateway, **Solana** effectively functioned as a top-five global exchange on day one. The network generated higher spot volumes than **Hyperliquid** and outpaced centralized heavyweights including **Kraken**, **KuCoin**, and **Bitget**. Crucially, **Solana** markets offered tighter spreads and deeper liquidity for MON than even the **Monad** native chain itself during the first 24 hours. This disrupts the traditional listing playbook, proving that high-performance chains can absorb and trade external volatility more efficiently than centralized order books living in data centers.

For institutional allocators, the implications are immediate: the "CEX-first" liquidity mandate is becoming obsolete. With protocols like **Mayan** already processing over $5 billion in cross-chain swaps and **Sunrise** enabling instant, permissionless market making, the friction of onboarding non-native assets has evaporated. We are witnessing the real-time validation of the "Internet's Original Sin" being corrected; **Solana** is not merely moving information, but is now the superior rail for moving money and forming capital markets that are globally competitive from minute one.
